paper: VeriEvol: Scaling Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning via Verifiable Evol-Instruct

claims.claim: Increasing evolved SFT data from 10K to 250K samples raises the five-benchmark mean accuracy from 35.42 to 54.73 (+19.31).

evidence.description: SFT data volume sweep: 10K→250K produces monotonic improvement from 35.42 to 54.73.
Data-volume scaling plot showing monotonic SFT scaling curve.

claims.notes: Clean isolation: same backbone, same SFT recipe, only sample count varies.

claims.claim: VeriEvol adds a cumulative +3.88 over an un-evolved RL baseline with backbone, SFT initialization, and GRPO recipe held fixed.

evidence.description: RL-stage ablation: RL-Origin 55.24 → RL-Evol 57.06 → RL-Evol+Verifier 59.12.

claims.notes: Pristine controlled comparison: all three RL rows share VeriEvol-SFT-Init and GRPO recipe.

claims.claim: Of the +3.88 RL-stage gain, +1.82 comes from evolved prompts and +2.06 from the HTV-Agent verifier.

evidence.description: RL-Origin→RL-Evol = +1.82 (evolved prompts); RL-Evol→RL-Evol+Verifier = +2.06 (verifier).

claims.notes: Marginal contributions are near-additive, supporting orthogonal failure modes claim.

claims.claim: Scaling verified RL data from 10K to 130K adds a smooth +4.60 along the data-volume axis.
claims.notes: Monotonic improvement, no benchmark worsens with scale.

claims.claim: On MathVerse-VO, VeriEvol (70.46) leads the strongest external baseline (OpenMMReasoner-7B, 63.80) by +6.66 points.

evidence.description: MathVerse-VO column: VeriEvol 70.46 vs OpenMMReasoner 63.80.

claims.notes: MathVerse-VO is the most diagnostic test of image reliance; VeriEvol targets this directly.

claims.claim: On DynaMath-Worst, full VeriEvol (35.73) leads every full-coverage external baseline (next-best OpenMMReasoner-7B, 34.90).

evidence.description: DynaMath-Worst column: VeriEvol 35.73 vs OpenMMReasoner 34.90.

claims.claim: Under We-Math-Strict, full VeriEvol (63.05) leads the strongest external strict score (OpenMMReasoner-7B, 53.81).

evidence.description: We-Math-Strict column: VeriEvol 63.05 vs OpenMMReasoner 53.81.

claims.notes: OpenMMReasoner's paper reports only loose metric; authors reproduced strict metric under their harness.

claims.claim: VeriEvol-SFT (VeriEvol-SFT-Init) lifts the five-benchmark mean from 51.80 to 54.73 over a Seed-only SFT baseline (+2.93).

evidence.description: Seed-only SFT 51.80 vs VeriEvol-SFT 54.73.

claims.claim: Prompt difficulty and answer reliability contributions are near-additive at the RL stage.

evidence.description: +1.82 (prompts) + 2.06 (verifier) = 3.88 which closely matches the cumulative gap RL-Origin→RL-Evol+Verifier of 3.88.

claims.notes: Near-additivity strongly suggests orthogonal failure modes but the SFT init interaction is acknowledged as a residual confound.

claims.claim: Evolved prompts sustain higher policy entropy throughout GRPO training (~0.31, roughly flat) whereas original prompts collapse to ~0.22.

evidence.description: GRPO training dynamics: entropy curves for RL-Origin vs RL-Evol.

claims.claim: Evolved prompts achieve higher terminal reward (running mean ~0.43 vs ~0.35).

evidence.description: GRPO reward curves for RL-Origin vs RL-Evol.

claims.claim: The full HTV-Agent improves inference-time judge accuracy by +4.51 pp over raw single-call baseline and never degrades any benchmark.

evidence.description: Raw 80.89 → Full HTV-Agent 85.40 (+4.51). All 6 benchmarks improve or stay flat.

claims.notes: Inference-time proxy on Gemini-3.5-Flash (300 samples/benchmark). Not a matched-budget RL ablation (acknowledged as Limitation ii.a).

claims.claim: Multi-solver self-consistency is the dominant component on visually rich tasks (MathVision: +6.00 pp from voting alone).

evidence.description: MathVision: Raw 83.33 → Single Solver 86.67 → SC 89.33 → SC+python_exec 90.14.

claims.claim: python_exec is the dominant component on computation-heavy tasks (OlympiadBench: +4.23 pp on top of SC).

evidence.description: OlympiadBench: SC 65.67 → SC+python_exec 69.90 (+4.23).

claims.claim: Two rounds of evolution at fixed 10K budget improve over one round (SFT: +1.37, RL: +0.50).

evidence.description: Evolution depth ablation at matched 10K budget.

claims.notes: SFT more sensitive to prompt quality; RL at 10K closer to saturation.

claims.claim: VG (visual grounding) and TS (text-only shortcut) errors drop from 57% to 38% of residual error pool with VeriEvol.

evidence.description: 60-sample pilot error distribution: VG+TS goes from 16/28 to 9/24 absolute.

claims.notes: 60-sample single-annotator pilot — illustrative, not significance-tested. Authors explicitly warn against quantitative conclusions.

claims.claim: In 12 paired flips, VeriEvol's successful traces show explicit image reference 83% of the time vs 42% for failed RL-Origin traces.

evidence.description: 12 paired flips analysed on 3 binary criteria.

claims.claim: VeriEvol SFT data scales monotonically across 6 volume points (10K→250K) without degradation.

evidence.description: 6-point sweep: every increment improves or is flat, never degrades.

claims.claim: VeriEvol RL data scales monotonically across 5 volume points (10K→130K) without any benchmark worsening.

evidence.description: 5-point sweep: every increment improves or is flat, no benchmark degrades.

claims.claim: VeriEvol is competitive on all five benchmarks and leads the mean against the strongest external 7B baselines.

evidence.description: Full external comparison against 16 baselines on 5 benchmarks.

claims.notes: External baselines from original papers (not re-run); OpenMMReasoner-7B uses 3.5× larger SFT corpus.

claims.claim: Verifier channels are complementary rather than redundant: SC helps visual tasks, python_exec helps computational tasks.

evidence.description: Per-benchmark inversion: SC dominant on MathVision, python_exec dominant on OlympiadBench.
